mission.title: Install and Configure Kubeflow
mission.description: Kubeflow is a machine learning toolkit for Kubernetes that provides a comprehensive set of tools for building, deploying, and managing machine learning workflows. It is ideal for teams looking to implement AI solutions at scale, leveraging Kubernetes' orchestration capabilities. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and management versus the flexibility and power of a fully integrated ML platform.

steps.title: Install the Kubeflow CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kubeflow CLI, which is necessary for managing your Kubeflow deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kubeflow/kubeflow/releases/download/v1.10.0/kubeflow-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xvf kubeflow-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv kubeflow /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the CLI for Linux
> Extract the downloaded archive
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster in your preferred cloud provider with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kubeflow-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Create a GKE cluster with 3 nodes

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kubeflow-cluster --region us-central1

```

> Update kubeconfig to use the new cluster
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials kubeflow-cluster --region us-central1

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in your cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes and their statuses

steps.title: Install Kubeflow
steps.description:
This step deploys Kubeflow to your Kubernetes cluster using the CLI.

```bash

kfctl apply -V -f https://github.com/kubeflow/manifests/archive/v1.10.0.tar.gz

```

> Deploy Kubeflow using the specified version

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures additional features such as autoscaling and monitoring.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubeflow/manifests/v1.10.0/monitoring/monitoring.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubeflow/manifests/v1.10.0/autoscaling/autoscaling.yaml

```

> Set up monitoring for Kubeflow
> Enable autoscaling for your deployments

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kubeflow
uninstall.description:
This step removes Kubeflow from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources, which may lead to data loss.

```bash

kfctl delete -V -f https://github.com/kubeflow/manifests/archive/v1.10.0.tar.gz

gcloud container clusters delete kubeflow-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

```

> Delete the GKE cluster and all associated resources
commands: gcloud container clusters delete kubeflow-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kubeflow
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your existing configurations and data. This step will upgrade your Kubeflow installation to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get all --all-namespaces > kubeflow-backup.yaml

kfctl apply -V -f https://github.com/kubeflow/manifests/archive/v1.10.0.tar.gz

kubectl get pods -n kubeflow-system

```

> Backup current resources
> Upgrade to the latest version of Kubeflow
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubectl CLI is not installed or not in your PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl using 'gcloud components install kubectl' or download it from the Kubernetes website.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is not running.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials kubeflow-cluster --region us-central1' to update kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'kubeflow-system' are in 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** One or more pods are failing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kubeflow-system' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources allocated for the workloads.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or resize the existing nodes in your cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new ML frameworks, improved integration with Kubernetes 1.30, and enhanced user interface.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Kubernetes versions below 1.27.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.9, ensure to update your configurations to align with the new API changes.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of running pods in the 'kubeflow-system' namespace when you run 'kubectl get pods -n kubeflow-system'.
codeSnippets: gcloud container clusters get-credentials kubeflow-cluster --region us-central1

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources and the necessary CLI tools installed.
